description_long: This object is a rectangular stone block with a series of evenly spaced, parallel grooves running across its top surface, giving it a ribbed appearance. The grooves are deep and dark, contrasting with the mottled, gray-black stone surface between them. The block is solid and robust, with sharp, clean edges and a uniform thickness. The coloration is predominantly dark gray with subtle variations, suggesting a natural stone material such as granite or basalt. The sides of the block are smooth and unadorned, while the top surface is the only area featuring the grooved pattern. This design is typical of urban infrastructure elements used for drainage, tactile paving, or as a deterrent barrier.
description: A dark, rectangular stone block with deep, parallel grooves across its top surface, creating a ribbed, textured appearance. The block is solid and robust, with a mottled gray-black coloration.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full width of the block and the parallel grooves running horizontally across the top surface.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth and spacing of the grooves, with the grooves running vertically from this perspective.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the parallel grooves across the top surface and the block's solid form.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again emphasizing the vertical grooves and the block's thickness.
attribute_location_context: urban sidewalk or street drainage area
attribute_installation_method: embedded in concrete or set flush with pavement
attribute_weathering_pattern: No significant weathering is visible; the surface appears clean and well-maintained with no chips or erosion.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: set flush with adjacent pavement or stone blocks
